{"key_event":"Student and pension protests reshape politics","reasoning":"Civil society became more powerful as student, environmental, feminist, indigenous, and No+AFP mobilizations repeatedly constrained governments and translated into electoral and reform gains, despite low trust and turnout. State capacity remained very strong and edged up through pension, tax, education, campaign-finance, and regulatory reforms, though legitimacy losses and Araucanía conflict limited any larger increase.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":8.1,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.9}
